chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ خَالَفَ الرَّمْيَ أَوْ زَادَ أَوْ نَقَصَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 175 – The one who pelts differently, or more, or less

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/151)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/151)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) ذَهَبْتُ أَرْمِي فَإِذَا فِي يَدِي سِتُّ حَصَيَاتٍ فَقَالَ خُذْ وَاحِدَةً مِنْ تَحْتِ رِجْلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘ I went over to pelt, but there were six pebbles in my hand’. So heasws said: ‘Take one from beneath your foot (but not from the Jamarah as per above Ahadith)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn abu Hamzah from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I went to throw pebbles but there were only six pebbles in my hand.’ He (the Imam) said, ’Take one from under your feet.’”
